title: Questions from Quarantine: The End of Summer and the Fall Ahead
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Security and Global Health Project hosts a weekly web series featuring experts Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux, who address public questions submitted with the hashtag #QuestionsFromQuarantine. The series, which airs every Tuesday, aims to provide guidance on maintaining mental and physical well-being during the ongoing global health crisis. The article promotes viewer engagement by encouraging the submission of questions and highlights related expert content on topics such as mega-event safety planning and global supply chains. It also previews upcoming content from the author on subjects including H3N2 influenza variants and the importance of sustained mRNA vaccine research funding.
result.main_content_zh: 安全与全球健康项目每周推出一档网络系列节目，由专家朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，回答公众通过#QuestionsFromQuarantine标签提交的疑问。该系列节目于每周二播出，旨在为全球健康危机期间如何保持身心健康提供建议。文章鼓励观众提交问题以促进互动，并推介了相关专家的其他内容，涉及大型活动安全规划、全球供应链等主题。同时，文章还预告了作者即将发布的后续内容，包括关于H3N2流感亚型变体的传播意义以及持续资助mRNA疫苗研究的重要性等议题。
